summary: Iteration 3 closes both remaining iteration-2 findings cleanly. F1 (resume vs. Done contradiction): the stray 'resumes' language is removed from R5 and R26, R5/R27 now name Done as the sole exit, and the assumptions section states explicitly that there is no Resume control and exactly two ways to leave the paused state (trigger a menu action, which returns to the paused menu still paused; or choose Done, which requires Define to be settled and finishes the phase) — the ambiguity is fully resolved with no residual 'resume' wording. F2 (in-flight display for single-step re-runs): new R12 specifies that while an Explore or Clarifying-Questions re-run is running, the menu is hidden and a live in-progress indicator is shown, matching R16's explicit 'restart in progress' treatment for the multi-step case, so every in-flight re-run is displayed consistently. The requirement set comprehensively covers the ask's user needs — pause anytime while Define runs (R1-R6), the five-item menu with each action's trigger, success display, and step-through-loop return (R7-R24), the failure path (R25-R26), Manager-race and retry-cap safeguards (R27-R30), and non-technical/existing-behavior integrity (R31-R34). Each requirement is testable and singular, holding the 'When X, Y should happen' shape. The only remaining edge — Ask Human's display when no open clarifying questions exist yet — is functionally covered by R14 (free-form notes are folded in as feedback regardless) and is a design-level empty-state detail rather than a business-requirement completeness gap, so it does not block convergence.
